observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant
to:
• submit to the Audit Team the Disbursement Vouchers and necessary
supporting documents of the claims listed in the Accounts Payable
totaling P10,909,529.63, if there is any, for our verification;
• ensure that the recording of liabilities is in accordance with Section 4(s)
of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
• ensure that all claims against government funds are properly supported
with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD No.
1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant
to make the necessary adjusting entries for the reclassification of the affected
accounts amounting to P1,741,625.00 and furnish the Audit Team with copies of
adjustments made for validation.

Thereafter, ensure that proper recording of entries was made in the books of
accounts for the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant
to:
• make the necessary adjustments on the affected accounts to recognize the
loss on disposal of unserviceable properties totaling P48,807.42;
• submit to the Audit Team the Journal Entry Voucher/s for the adjustments made; and
• strictly comply with the provisions on gain or loss on disposal of PPEs
stated in Paragraphs 83 and 86 of IPSAS 17.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant
to cause the immediate submission of Approved Contract Time Extensions,
Suspensions Orders and Resumption Orders issued relative to the two
infrastructure projects together with the contractors’ request for extension and
justification and the Municipal Engineer’s Investigation Reports as basis in
approving time extensions and suspensions, if there is any. Otherwise, impose
liquidated damages to the contractors who incurred delays in completing the
project.

Also, ensure that all future disbursements pertaining to infrastructure projects
were properly supported with complete documentation in compliance with
Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445.

Henceforth, require the Municipal Engineering Office to properly supervise the
implementation of infrastructure projects and to ensure that projects will be
satisfactorily completed within the specified target date.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
submit to the Audit Team all the required supporting documents for evaluation.

Also, ensure that all claims against government funds are properly supported
with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer and
the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to:
• submit the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities
(“PPA”) and those that are to be implemented during the year and its
monthly monitoring report within the prescribed period, and
• ensure that signboards of all infrastructure projects of the Municipality are
provided with the relevant information in compliance with COA Circular No.
2013-004.

observations.managementAction:
The Disposal finished the appraisal and inspection of the unserviceable properties.
The Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties were submitted and the supporting documents were submitted to the Audit Team.
The unserviceable properties were disposed by the Municipality through sale and destruction.
The Accounting Office made necessary adjusting entries on disposal of properties.
observations.reason: The net book value of disposed properties remained in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to continue exerting extra effort in retrieving the documents required by the Register of Deeds to facilitate the immediate titling and registration of the lots in the name of the Municipality to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future and in compliance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
Also, seek the assistance of the Provincial Legal Officer/Prosecutor on matters pertaining to properties with legal issues in accordance with Section 31 of Article III of Republic Act No. 7160 or the Local Government Code of 1991 in the absence of Municipal Legal Officer.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality is doing their best to facilitate the immediate titling and registration of lands included in the observation.
For those portion of land previously acquired by the Municipality, they are planning to hire a Private Geodetic Engineer that will help them to have an Approved Subdivision Plan to be able to comply with the requirements of the Registry of Deeds for the issuance of title for the municipality.
The Municipality also seek the assistance of the Provincial Legal Officer/Prosecutor on matters pertaining to legal issues of the untitled land.
observations.reason: The parcels of land remained unsupported with Certificate of Title in favor of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Treasurer had adopt efficient and aggressive collection strategies and enforce the repayment terms offered by the Municipality to ensure recovery of the amounts from the beneficiaries. Also, require the Municipal Accountant to set up the provision for Allowance for Impairment in order to present fairly the Loans Receivable-Others account at net realizable amount consistent with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 and in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer had adopted efficient collection strategies.
The Municipal Treasurer had granted write-off of accounts for various debtors outstanding already and adjustments made by the Municipal Accountant on an amount of P2,900.00.
An allowance for impairment totaling P30,490.00 was already setup per JEV no. 400-2021-05-182 dated May 21, 2021.
observations.reason: A balance of P54,980.00 remained uncollected as of December 31, 2021. P268,180.00 was granted per Decision No. 2021-001 dated November 17, 2021. A total of P7,400.00 was collected by the Municipal Treasurer and adjustments was made by the Municipal Accountant on an amount of P2,900.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that henceforth the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all cash advances are liquidated within the prescribed period in accordance with existing rules and regulations for the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant see to it that cash advances were fully liquidated at year-end, hence there is no outstanding cash advances as of December 31, 2021.

The Municipality implemented the GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 and saw to it that grants, utilization, and liquidation of cash advances are in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002.
The Municipality continue to adhere with the procurement procedures provided in the RA No. 9184 and the GPPB Circular No. 01-2020. Some procurements which were for immediate need were still made through reimbursement but the Municipality see to it the purchase is properly supported with complete documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Audit Team all the required supporting documents for evaluation. Otherwise, the same may be suspended or disallowed in audit. Also, ensure that all claims against government funds are properly supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted to the Audit Team all the required supporting documents which were found complete and supporting documents which were found incomplete and the same were suspended for further evaluation. Also, ensure that all claims against government funds are properly supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.reason: Various disbursements for CY 2021 were not supported with complete documentation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• require the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team all the required supporting documents for the evaluation. Otherwise, the same may be suspended or disallowed in audit;
• require the Municipal Treasurer to require the personnel of National Government Agencies (BFP/PNP) to refund the payment of Hazard Pay of P84,150.00 made to them. They can claim from their parent agency (BFP/PNP) in accordance with Item 4.7 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2020-1 which provides that, “The COVID-19 Hazard Pay of an employee on detail to another government agency shall be granted by the parent agency”.; and
• ensure that all claims against government funds are properly supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction:
The payment was refunded per Official Receipt Nos. 3661038 to 3661040 totaling P6,230.32.
The Municipal Accountant will submit to the Audit Team all the necessary supporting documents.
Also, ensure that all claims against government funds are properly supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.reason: Various disbursements for CY 2021 were not supported with complete documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team all the required supporting documents for evaluation. Otherwise, the same may be suspended or disallowed in audit. Henceforth, ensure that all claims against government funds are properly supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to require the personnel of the National Government Agencies (BFP/PNP) to refund the payment of Hazard Pay of P84,150.00 made to them. They can claim from their parent agency (BFP/PNP) in accordance with Item 4.7 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2020-1.
observations.managementAction:
The personnel of the National Government Agencies had refunded the Hazard Pay amounting to P84,150.00.
Management agreed with the audit recommendations and the Municipal Accountant will submit to the Audit Team all the necessary supporting documents.
observations.reason: Various disbursements for CY 2021 were not supported with complete documentation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• direct the Municipal Officials and Employees to refrain from procuring goods and services through reimbursement and ensure strict implementation of RA No. 9184 in order to prevent abuses and corruption in the procurement process;
• instruct the Municipal Accountant to see to it the disbursement vouchers were supported with approved APP/PPMP; and
• require the Bids and Awards Committee to adhere to the procurement procedures provided in the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (RIRR) of RA No. 9184 and to ensure that procurements are properly supported with complete documentation.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality tried their best to lessen the payment of reimbursements specially those procurement which are emergency in nature. Nevertheless, there are instances like emergency purchases that they instantly need to purchase the materials, hence they resorted to reimbursement. The Municipality ensure that the procurement is properly supported with complete documentation.
The paid Disbursement Vouchers were supported with approved APP/PPMP.
observations.reason: Some procurements were still made through reimbursement. The disbursement vouchers were not supported with Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and/or Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP), hence the legality and validity of the procurements made through reimbursement cannot be ascertained.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor:
• require the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to finish the appraisal,
inspection and disposal of these unserviceable properties;
• require the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to submit to the Audit Team
the required Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property
(IIRUP) with their technical description together with its supporting
documents for the evaluation of unserviceable properties;
• cause the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties in order to
maximize any realizable value from their disposal by sale and the
derecognition of the same for the fair presentation of the financial
statements;
• require the Accounting Office to prepare the necessary adjusting entries
after the disposal; and
• ensure that the disposal of property either thru public bidding or negotiated
sale is properly documented and duly authorized.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
Municipal Assessor to continue exerting extra effort in retrieving the documents
required by the Register of Deeds to facilitate the immediate titling and
registration of the lots in the name of the Municipality to avoid contingent claims
that may arise in the future and in compliance with Section 39(2) of PD No.1445
and Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 and for the fair presentation of the
Land account in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of
IPSAS 1.

Also, seek the assistance of the Provincial Legal Officer/Prosecutor on matters
pertaining to properties with legal issues in accordance with Section 31 of
Article III of Republic Act No. 7160 or the Local Government Code of 1991 in the
absence of Municipal Legal Officer.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Office
of the Municipal Treasurer to continue adopting efficient and aggressive
collection strategies and enforce the repayment terms offered by the Municipality
to ensure recovery of the amounts from the existing beneficiaries.

Also, require the Municipal Accountant to set up the provision for Allowance for
Impairment in order to present fairly the Loans Receivable-Others account of
P327,660.00 in the financial statements at net realizable amount consistent with the
requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 and in accordance with Paragraph 27
of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that henceforth the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to ensure that all cash advances are liquidated within the prescribed
period in accordance with existing rules and regulations for the fair presentation
of the accounts in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of
IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to refrain from
procuring materials, supplies and equipment through cash advances;
• require the Municipal Accountant to record the purchases of
supplies/inventories initially under the appropriate asset account, and
recognize the expense account once the inventories are
consumed/distributed in accordance with IPSAS 12;
• instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to adhere to the procurement
procedures provided in GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 and strictly implement
the use of the alternative methods of procurement provided in the Revised
Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No. 9184, if public bidding is not
feasible; and
• ensure that the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances is in
accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
submit to the Audit Team all the required supporting documents for evaluation.
Otherwise, the same may be suspended or disallowed in audit.

Also, ensure that all claims against government funds are properly supported
with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• require the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team all the
required supporting documents for the procurements totaling P666,476.75
and the 12 unsubmitted disbursement vouchers totaling P557,249.00 for
evaluation. Otherwise, the same may be suspended or disallowed in audit;
• instruct the Municipal Treasurer to require the payee/officers and
employees to refund the payment totaling P6,230.32 for disbursements not
allowed to be charged against the BGCM as stated in Item 3.6 of DBM Budget
Circular No. 125 dated April 7, 2020; and
• ensure that all claims against government funds are properly supported
with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD No.
1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
submit to the Audit Team all the required supporting documents for evaluation.
Otherwise, the same may be suspended or disallowed in audit.

Henceforth, ensure that all claims against government funds are properly
supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD
No. 1445.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to require the personnel of the National Government Agencies
(BFP/PNP) to refund the payment of Hazard Pay of P84,150.00 made to them.
They can claim from their parent agency (BFP/PNP) in accordance with Item 4.7
of DBM Budget Circular No. 2020-1 which provides that, “The COVID-19 Hazard
Pay of an employee on detail to another government agency shall be granted by
the parent agency”.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Officials and
Employees to refrain from procuring goods and services through
reimbursement and ensure strict implementation of RA No. 9184 in order to
prevent abuses and corruption in the procurement process.

Also, instruct the Municipal Accountant to see to it the disbursement vouchers
for procurement of goods and services were duly supported with approved
APP/PPMP.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to continue exerting extra effort in retrieving the documents required by the Register of Deeds to facilitate the immediate titling and registration of the lots in the name of the Municipality to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future and in compliance with Section 39(2) of PD No.1445 and Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 and for the fair presentation of the Land account in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
Also, seek the assistance of the Provincial Legal Officer/Prosecutor on matters pertaining to properties with legal issues in accordance with Section 31 of Article III of Republic Act No. 7160 or the Local Government Code of 1991 in the absence of Municipal Legal Officer.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Assessor commented that they are in the process of tracing all the original documents/conveyances required by the Bureau of Internal Revenue for the payment of corresponding taxes due to BIR. It is just recently that she found out that most of the Deed of Conveyances do not have the issuance of Certificate Authorizing Registration from the BIR. This certificate is one of the major requirements for the transfer of ownership. To prevent conflicts and claims, the Municipality is already looking for a competent Geodetic Engineer for the relocation and verification survey of the properties while they are in the process of the land transfer. They will continue to make extra effort for the immediate titling and registration of the stated parcels of land.
Also, they will seek the assistance from private legal firms pertaining to legal matters.
observations.reason: Retrieving of necessary documents and titling of lots are still in process. The COVID-19 Pandemic has disrupted the day to day activity and transactions of the Municipality.

observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer commented that the members of the Appraisal and Disposal Committee had submitted the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) with technical description together with its supporting documents and justification. Also, thru Sangguniang Bayan Resolution, all unserviceable vehicles were disposed thru negotiated sale. Official Receipts will be submitted to the Audit Team. For those other unserviceable properties that have been unaccounted for and of no value, they will remove these properties from the inventory list of the Municipality. Disposal of property of vehicle was done thru negotiated sale.
The Municipal Accountant commented that adjusting entries had been made on April 23, 2021, as per Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) Nos. 100-2021-04-1189/1193;1194;1195 and 1196 after the disposal of vehicles and on unserviceable asset waiting for disposal.
observations.reason: The appraisal and inspection of unserviceable properties is still on-going. The implementation of community quarantine during CY 2020 due to COVID-19 affected the operation of the Municipality; thus, disposal of unserviceable properties was not fully completed during CY 2020. The Appraisal and Disposal Committee have completed the IIRUP but the IIRUP together with its supporting documents for the total amount was not yet submitted to the Audit Team since they are still completing the necessary details and documents to support the IIRUP. Adjusting entries were prepared later (April 23, 2021) as claimed by Management; documentation to be furnished to Audit Team for validation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to continue adopting efficient and aggressive collection strategies and enforce the repayment terms offered by the Municipality to ensure recovery of the amounts from the existing beneficiaries.
Also, require the Municipal Accountant to set up the provision for Allowance for Impairment in order to present fairly the Loans Receivable-Others account in the financial statements at net realizable amount consistent with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 and in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer commented that they are continuously making follow-up of the payment from the beneficiaries. The Municipal Accountant commented that they set-up an allowance for impairment on May 21, 2021 as per Journal Entry Voucher No. 400-2021-05-182 dated May 21, 2021 in the amount of P298,974.00.
observations.reason: Most of the borrower/beneficiaries were not able and cannot pay the said loan. The Municipal Treasurer sent demand letters with Return Card and was able to collect P5,800.00 in CY 2020. The allowance for impairment was set up in CY 2021 (after the reporting date).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the Municipal Engineer to:
• submit to the Supervising Auditor and the Audit Team Leader at the beginning of the year, the complete list of all ongoing projects/programs/activities and the complete list of all ongoing during the year with relevant information as required in Item 2.1 of COA Circular 2013-004;
• inform the Audit Team within 10 days after the award of the project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices were already posted for prompt validation and inspection of the Audit Team; and
• regularly submit the monthly monitoring report with supporting documents in compliance with the existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the Municipal Engineer submitted to the Supervising Auditor and the Audit Team Leader at the beginning of the year, the complete list of all ongoing projects/programs/activities and the complete list of all ongoing during the year. They regularly informed the Audit Team that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices were already posted. They regularly submit the monthly monitoring report with supporting documents in compliance with the existing regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to expedite the completion of Community-Based Monitoring System that will establish/create a GAD Database that will assist the gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator had completed the Community-Based Monitoring System and the GAD Focal Point System had been established and created the GAD Database.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Assessor to continue exerting extra effort in retrieving the documents required by the
Register of Deeds to facilitate the immediate titling and registration of the lots in the
name of the Municipality to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future and in
compliance with Section 39(2) of PD No.1445 and Section 148 of COA Circular No.
92-386 and for the fair presentation of the Land account in the financial statements as
required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• require the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to finish the appraisal,
inspection and disposal of these unserviceable properties;
• require the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to complete and accomplish the
required Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property
(IIRUP) with their technical description for the evaluation of unserviceable
properties;
• submit to the COA Audit Team the IIRUP together with its supporting
documents;
• cause the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties in order to
maximize any realizable value from their disposal by sale and the
derecognition of the same for the fair presentation of the financial
statements;
• require the Accounting Office to prepare the necessary adjusting entries; and
• ensure that the disposal of property either thru public bidding or negotiated sale is
properly documented and duly authorized.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Office of the Municipal
Treasurer to continue adopting efficient and aggressive collection strategies
and enforce the repayment terms offered by the Municipality to ensure recovery
of the amounts from the existing beneficiaries.

Also, require the Municipal Accountant to set up the provision for Allowance for
Impairment in order to present fairly the Loans Receivable-Others account in the
financial statements at net realizable amount consistent with the requirements of COA
Circular No. 2016-005 and for the fair presentation of the account in the financial
statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and
Development Officer and the Municipal Engineer to:
• submit to the Supervising Auditor and the Audit Team Leader at the
beginning of the year, the complete list of all ongoing government
projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during
the year with relevant information as required in Item 2.1 of COA Circular
2013-004;
• inform the Audit Team within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure
project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project
signboards and/or public notices were already posted for prompt validation
and inspection of the Audit Team; and
• regularly submit the monthly monitoring report with supporting documents
in compliance with the existing regulations.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to exert extra effort in retrieving the documents required by the Register of Deeds to facilitate the titling in process.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor designated the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the titling of the untitled properties of the Municipality. On December 12, 2019, the Municipal Treasurer fully turned over to the Municipal Assessor all the documents that she had pertaining to untitled properties of the Municipality and provide her updates on the status of the remaining untitled lots for the application of title. The Municipal Assessor requested for the status of each parcel of land from DENR in Pagbilao, Quezon. She was able to retrieve various documents pertaining to the titling of lots such as technical description and cadastral map of each untitled parcel of land. Also, she has been to Land Registration Authority in Quezon City for the verification of subdivision entry for the portion of land owned by the Municipality, and has been to DENR Records Office in Los Baños and DENR Regional Office No. IV-A in Calamba City, Laguna to inquire regarding the 20 parcels of land to be transferred in the name of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Retrieving of necessary documents and titling of lots are still in process.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• require the Municipal Accountant to record all the vehicles which are whether serviceable or unserviceable to the appropriate asset accounts in compliance with PPSAS 17 for proper presentation in the financial statements;
• require the Appraisal committee to expedite the appraisal of the vehicles to determine the fair values at acquisition dates; and
• require the Municipal Treasurer to locate the missing documents and continue the exerting more efforts to facilitate the registration and transfer of the Motor Vehicles in the name of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already recorded in the books of accounts all the vehicles which are registered in the name of the Municipality. The Appraisal Committee finished the appraisal of the vehicles and submitted the report to the COA Audit Team. The Municipal Treasurer exerted efforts in locating missing documents but to no avail and to know the whereabouts of Mr. Orlando Honrade, the Municipal Administrator at time of donation of service vehicle. The subject vehicles are not yet disposed of but already Included in the IIRUP. The Disposal Committee are still completing the necessary documents prior to its disposal.
observations.reason: The subject vehicles are not yet disposed of but the same were included in the report because the same were included in the records of the Municipal Accounting Office; the Appraisal and Disposal Committee required all the Department Heads to submit a certification/justification on the unserviceable properties which are not physically available but are listed in the IIRUP in their respective offices. With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew under audit observation no. 2, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to continue adopting efficient and aggressive collection strategies and enforce the repayment terms offered by the Municipality to ensure recovery of the amounts from the existing beneficiaries. We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to determine those receivables the collection thereof is not anymore feasible despite diligent efforts in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer and request for write-off of receivables be filed with the Commission on Audit in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer discussed the matter with the Municipal Agricultural Office and the Municipal Planning Office and they came up with a hand-in-hand joint efforts and strategies to enforce the collection of loan balances to borrowers/beneficiaries. She assured that they will continue to send demand letters through post office and will conduct one-on-one discussion for payment arrangement once the COVID-19 pandemic is over. Further, the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) passed Resolution No. 2017-148 dated November 26, 2018 approving the request for write-off of the Accounts Receivables for unpaid obligations of beneficiaries who passed away and can no longer pay the amount due from them. The request for write-off including the SB Resolution and other supporting documents were submitted to the Audit Team during the latter part of CY 2019 but some documents needed were not submitted on time, hence causing the delay in the evaluation of the submitted reports.
observations.reason: Most of the borrower/beneficiaries were not able and cannot pay the said loan. With the new developments, the audit observation is presented anew under audit observation no. 3, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer/Special Disbursing Officer to immediately settle their cash advances, not otherwise, refunding at year end the excess amount of cash advances and drawing additional cash advances, contrary to COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and PD No. 1445, thus resulted to overstatement of the asset account and understatement of related expenses equivalent to the amount of unreported disbursements. Also, implement the withholding of her salary to be applied to her accounts; and ensure the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of all cash advances within the prescribed period in accordance with the existing rules and regulation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer already submitted the Liquidation Report to the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to exert extra effort to enforce massive and aggressive collection and adopt the remedies to enforce collections of delinquent real property tax payers in accordance with RA No. 7160. We suggested the following strategies: 1. Posting list of delinquent real property taxes in accordance of the Municipality bulletin board; 2. Conducting tax campaign at barangay level; 3. Serving/drawing letter to taxpayers; 4. Conduct of dialogue with concerned taxpayers and barangay officials; 5. Recommend for the issuance of warrant of levy for action.
observations.managementAction: As a result, total amount of P1,333,098.04 from RPT delinquencies were collected during the year. These collection strategies are still being applied by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: • ensure that the Property Acknowledgement Receipt is renewed every three years or whenever there is a change in custodianship, in compliance with Section 492 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM), Volume I, in order that record of accountability for PPEs is updated; and • assign a responsible person in the renewal of PAR every three years after issuance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer see to it that Property Acknowledgement Receipt is renewed every three years. The Municipal Treasurer assigned one personnel in-charge in the renewal of PAR every three years after issuance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to expedite the completion of Community-Based Monitoring System that will establish/create a GAD Database that will assist the gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator explained that in CY 2019, they have submitted the list of projects to be implemented to the previous Audit Team which include only those infrastructure projects being funded by the 20% Development Fund and grants from National Government Agencies (NGAs). Due to multifarious functions and duties assigned to her and with limited technical staff, infrastructure projects with minimal cost and non-infrastructure PPAs were not given much attention, hence not included in the submitted report. As a remedy, she recommended to the Local Chief Executive to issue memorandum to all department heads to prepare their own reports for consolidation by the Office of the MPDC instead. Copies of the memorandum issued by the Municipal Mayor and duly received by various department heads and BAC Secretariat were submitted to the Audit Team. Nevertheless, she commented that the Municipality committed to abide with the audit recommendations starting this year and the ensuing years. (Note: Elsewhere in the report MPDC conducted 3-day workshop and as of Dec. 31, 2019 eleven (11) out of fourteen (14) barangays submitted sex disaggregated data.)
observations.reason: Three barangays have not yet submitted their report on sex disaggregated data, hence hindering the completion of CBMS and GAD database. With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew under audit observation no. 5, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
Municipal Assessor to exert extra effort in retrieving the documents required
by the Register of Deeds to facilitate the immediate titling and registration of
the lots in the name of the Municipality to avoid contingent claims that may
arise in the future and in compliance with Section 39(2) of PD No. 1445 and
Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 and for the fair presentation of the
Land account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a) Municipal Accountant to record all the vehicles which are already
registered in the name of the Municipality whether serviceable or
unserviceable to the appropriate asset accounts in compliance with
PPSAS 17 for proper presentation in the financial statements;
b) Appraisal Committee to expedite the appraisal of the vehicles to
determine the fair values at acquisition dates for recording purposes; and
c) Municipal Treasurer
 to locate the missing documents and continue exerting more effort to
facilitate the registration and transfer of the Motor Vehicles in the
name of the Municipality in accordance with Section 7 of RA No.
4136.
 to closely monitor the activities on the disposal processes to ensure
that these are fully complied with especially the documentary
requirement thereby enabling them to effect the immediate disposal
of the unserviceable properties in order to maximize any realizable
value from their disposal by sale.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Office
of the Municipal Treasurer to continue adopting efficient and aggressive
collection strategies and enforce the repayment terms offered by the
Municipality to ensure recovery of the amounts from the beneficiaries.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to determine those receivables the collection thereof is not
anymore feasible despite diligent efforts in coordination with the Municipal
Treasurer and request for write-off of receivables be filed with the Commission
on Audit in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19,
2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant
to:
 send written notice or demand to the Accountable Officer to immediately
settle her cash advances, otherwise, implement the withholding of her
salary to be applied to her accounts; and
 ensure the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of all cash advances
within the prescribed period in accordance with the existing rules and
regulation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to
exert extra effort to enforce massive and strategize collections and adopt the
remedies to enforce collections of delinquent real property taxes in
accordance with RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to:
 ensure that Property Acknowledgement Receipt is renewed every three
years or whenever there is a change in custodianship, in compliance with
Section 492 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM),
Volume I, in order that record of accountability for PPEs is updated; and
 assign a responsible person in the renewal of PAR every three years after
issuance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator to expedite the completion of Community-Based
Monitoring System that will establish/create a GAD Database that will assist
the gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation of the
Municipality.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to continue the work of the Municipal Treasurer and exert extra effort in retrieving the documents required by Register of Deeds to facilitate the immediate titling of subject lots and registration in the name of the Municipality to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future and in compliance with Section 39(2) of PD No. 1445 and Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor received an Office Order by the Municipal Mayor dated September 11, 2018
observations.reason: Some of the documents required by the Register of Deeds need to be retrieved from the sellers of the subject lots.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor require the: a) Municipal Accountant to record all the vehicles which are already registered in the name of the Municipality whether serviceable or unserviceable to the appropriate asset accounts in compliance with PPSAS 17 for proper presentation in the financial statements; b) Appraisal Committee to expedite the appraisal of the vehicles to determine the fair values at acquisition dates for recording purposes; c) Designated Property Custodian to prepare Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) for the disposal of unserviceable motor vehicles in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445; and d) Municipal Treasurer to continue exerting more effort to facilitate the registration and transfer of the Motor Vehicles in the name of the Municipality in accordance with Section 7 of RA No. 4136 and to locate the missing documents to expedite the registration and transfer of the same.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer stated that they are in the process of registration and transfer of motor vehicle in the name of the Municipality, however their efforts appeared to be futile with regard to the vehicles donated by the MWSS because of the requirement by the LTO for the MWSS Administrator for which two valid IDs and signature were required was not residing in the Philippines and they cannot find any information on how to contact him. In addition she also stated that the Appraisal and Disposal Committee have scheduled the process of disposal of unserviceable vehicles on July 2019 and she is exerting efforts to locate the missing documents to facilitate the registration and transfer of the Motor Vehicles in the name of the Municipality. Designated Property Custodian already prepared Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP).
observations.reason:
Lack of documents that will establish the ownership of the vehicles.
There are still motor vehicles not yet registered in the name of the Municipality.
With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew in Audit Observation No. 2 Pages No. 31-34 Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to continue adopting efficient and aggressive collection strategies and enforce the repayment terms offered by the Municipality to ensure recovery of the amounts from the beneficiaries.
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to determine those receivables the collection thereof is not anymore feasible despite diligent efforts in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer and request for write-off of receivables be filed with the Commission on Audit in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer stated that they are adopting efficient and aggressive collection strategies and enforce the repayment term by sending Demand Letters to the beneficiaries with existing loans, visited and talked with them on the matters, attended meetings of their organizations and discuss with them agreement on the schedule of payment of their amortization. Further, she also stated that they assigned staff to monitor and collect their payments. In addition she also stated that most of the livelihood beneficiaries are already old and they cannot afford to pay their loans as they are depending their lives to their children. Lastly, she also stated that in CY 2018, the Sangguniang Barangay passed a Resolution No. 2017-107 dated November 26, 2018 approving the request for write off of the accounts receivable from farmers under the Unlad Kabuhayan Program of the late Governor Rafael P. Nantes.
observations.reason: Majority of the debtors are no longer working, thus making the collection more difficult.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to:
• exert more effort to enforce massive and strategize collections of delinquent real property taxes by following remedies to enforce collection of delinquent real property taxes: a. Conduct house to house visitation/tax campaign; b. Conduct information dissemination thru Barangay Assembly; c. Send Notice of Delinquency to delinquent RPT taxpayers; d. Publish/Post unsettled delinquencies to conspicuous places; • Partially monitor the payment of real property taxes due for the current year and update the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR), or impose the available remedies provided for under the Local Government Code.
Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to submit up-to-date Assessment Roll to the Municipal Treasurer for them to keep their records reconciled at all times and to regularly monitor the status of collection of current and delinquent real property taxes.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer exerted extra effort and provided the following remedies to enforce collection of taxes: a. Conduct house to house visitation/tax campaign; b. Conduct information dissemination thru Barangay Assembly; c. Send Notice of Delinquency to delinquent RPT taxpayers; d. Publish/Post unsettled delinquencies to conspicuous places. The Municipal Treasurer served a warrant of levy to 5 delinquent taxpayers and collected the amount of P1,333,098.04 out of delinquent taxpayers. The Municipal Assessor already submitted up-to-date Assessment Roll to the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to continue to update the current and previous taxable parcels for purposes in compliance with Section 219 of the Local Government Code of 1991 to generate additional income from increased tax assessment.
observations.managementAction: Complied with regulations provided in Section 219 of the Local Government Code of 1991.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer for the immediate implementation of the project.
observations.managementAction: Implementation of the project was already made.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor: • make a representation to the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) for the enactment or approval of the resolution for the creation of economic enterprises of the LGU; and • require the Municipal Accountant to maintain a complete set of subsidiary ledgers for each economic enterprise operated by the Municipality and prepare the required Financial Statements and supporting schedules in compliance with Section 313 of RA 7160 and Sections 105 and 110 of the MNGAS. We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer adopt other measures that would improve the income generation and ensure that collections/income is properly accounted for.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor made representation and the resolution for the creation of economic enterprises was enacted. The Municipal Accountant already maintains a complete set of subsidiary ledgers for each economic enterprise. The Municipal Treasurer adopted other measures that improve the income generation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipality, through the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, prepare its updated 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan and secure its approval from the National Solid Waste Management Commission.
observations.managementAction: Solid Waste Management Plan was already submitted to DENR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO to submit the Accomplishment Report for DRRM Funds to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Accomplishment Report for DRRM Funds was already submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: Municipal Accountant to record all the vehicles acquired through purchase or through donation whether serviceable or unserviceable to the appropriate asset accounts in compliance with Section 112 of P.D. No. 1445 for proper presentation in the financial statement
observations.reason: Absence of documents that will established the ownership of the vehicles. [Reiterated in Observation No. 2, Part II of this report]

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to continue exerting extra effort in retrieving documents required by the Register of Deeds to facilitate the immediate titling of the subject lots and registration in the name of the Municipality to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future and in compliance with Section 39(2) of PD No. 1445 and Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
Management Comment
The Municipal Treasurer commented that during 2016 two (2) parcels of land were already titled in the name of the Municipality. The Municipal Treasurer also verified the three lots in the name of the private persons and it was found out that these lots have an existing tax declarations in the name of the Municipality. The Municipal Treasurer also mentioned that they already forwarded a letter to the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) regarding this matter. In addition, some lots are already in the name of the Schools and it has deed of donations as supporting documents. For the remaining untitled lots, the Municipal Treasurer said that they are continuously exerting effort to facilitate its titling.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant to prepare the proper accounting entries for the properties already transferred in the name of the schools and submit to the Audit Team the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) together with the supporting documents. Also provide the School Head of the schools of the copy of JEVs for the proper recording in their books. Moreover, it is requested that copy of the Transfer Certificate of Title of those properties already in the name of the Municipality be submitted to us.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the:
Municipal Accountant to record all the vehicles which are already registered in the name of the Municipality whether serviceable or unserviceable to the appropriate asset accounts in compliance with PPSAS 17 for proper presentation in the financial statements
Appraisal Committee to expedite the appraisal of the vehicles to determine the fair values at acquisition dates for recording purposes;
Designated Property Custodian to prepare Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) for the disposal of unserviceable motor vehicles in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445.
Municipal Treasurer to continue exerting more effort to facilitate the registration and transfer of the above Motor Vehicles in the name of the Municipality in accordance with Section 7 of RA No. 4136 and to locate the missing documents to expedite the registration and transfer of the same.
Management Comment
The Municipal Treasurer commented that for the donated vehicles, they do not have any supporting documents that will established their ownership regarding the said vehicles. She added that an inquiry was also made to the donor for any available documents that will support the donations of the vehicles, however there was none. The Municipal Treasurer also commented that they already conducted the appraisal and prepared the IIRUP.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team further recommended that the IIRUP be submitted to this office. Also, it is recommended to cause the proper disposal of the unserviceable properties even though they are not yet registered in the name of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to continue adopting efficient and aggressive collection strategies to enforce collection of the loan receivable accounts from the concerned beneficiaries to ensure the continuity of the livelihood project and give other prospective beneficiaries equal opportunity in availing capital for the livelihood program.  Also, if all means failed, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor make a representation to the Sangguniang Bayan for the write off of the accounts receivables to the Commission on Audit regarding unpaid obligations of one farmer who passed away and can no longer pay the amount due to them, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.
Management Comment
The Municipal Treasurer explained that they already have copies of the death certificates of the borrowers who passed away. Currently they are still exerting efforts to collect their receivables. The Municipal Mayor added that they will exert effort to the best of their ability to comply with the recommendation.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
We recommended further that a request for write-off of receivables with valid documents to support the claim be filed with the Commission on Audit in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 for the accounts of those who are already deceased.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:
exert more effort to enforce massive and strategize collections of delinquent real property taxes by conducting tax campaign and offering incentives of full payment of tax due.
monitor the payment of real property taxes due for the current year and update the RPTAR, or impose the available remedies provided for under the Local Government Code.
Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor to keep their records reconciled at all times to regularly monitor the status of collection of current and delinquent real property taxes.
Management Comment:
According to the Municipal Treasurer, reconciliation with the Municipal Assessor is already ongoing. She also mentioned that the reconciliation made them discover new Real Property Units (RPUs) causing their receivables to increase. Currently they are still in the process of reconciliation because they found out that some of the properties were duplicated, some were not cancelled and some were already consolidated. The Municipal Assessor, on the other hand, explained that they can cancel some duplicated properties if the supporting documents were adequate. She added that they are also doing a tax mapping.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team further advised that cancellation be made for the duplicated properties and update their records to facilitate the reconciliation.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to continue the representation with the Sangguniang Panlalawigan for the earliest approval of their request to conduct the general revision of assessment in order to update the current and previous taxable parcels for valuation and tax purposes in compliance with Section 219 of the Local Government Code of 1991 and to generate additional income from increased tax assessment.
Management Comment
The Municipal Assessor commented that the revision is a Provincial Imposition and they already submitted a proposal for the revision of assessment.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team requested for the submission of copy of the transmittal letter for the proposal submitted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to adjust the Accounts Payable in accordance with the 2009 RRSA.
Management Comment:
The Municipal Accountant committed that she will make an adjusting entry in compliance with the recommendation.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
It is further requested that a copy of the JEVs for the adjusting entry be submitted at the Office of the Auditor.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:
make a representation to the SB for the enactment or approval of the resolution for the creation of economic enterprises of the Municipality; and
require the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete sets of subsidiary ledgers for each economic enterprise operated by the Municipality and prepare the required Financial Statements and supporting schedules in compliance with Section 313 of RA 7160 and Sections 105 and 110 of the MNGAS.
We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer adopt other measures that would improve the income generation and ensure that collections/income are properly accounted for.
Management Comment
The Municipal Accountant committed that she will coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer and SB with regard to their compliance with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the members of the Municipal School Board to:
a. prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization;
b. refrain from accommodating expenditures for activities for which the  DepEd has the direct mandate to execute;
c. request for input from the DepEd officials or Schools Division Superintendent in the prioritization of SEF projects and expenditures in accordance with the DepEd criteria and with due regard to the provisions of laws, rules and regulations prescribing the limitations on the use of the fund; and
d. maximize the utilization of SEF to attain its objectives, monitor the implementation of the projects/programs and activities (PPAs), and take appropriate actions such as realignment or reprogramming, if necessary.
Management Comment:
The Municipal Mayor and the Sangguniang Bayan committed that they will do their best to maximize the use of SEF and will observe compliance with the guidelines in the utilization of SEF.
Auditors Rejoinder:
The Audit Team further recommended that a balanced budget must be observed in the utilization of the SEF. Coordinate with the DepEd Schools Division Superintendent in order to identify the planned expenditures already charged to the Department of Education.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the requirements stated under COA Circular No. 92-389 to prevent the possible re-use of those documents for other claims of same nature as part of a sound internal control system in the disbursement of funds.
Management Comment:
The Municipal Treasurer commented that they already started their compliance with the recommendation in May 2017.

We recommended that the Municipality, through the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, prepare its Solid Waste Management Plan for the next ten years and secure its approval from the National Solid Waste Management Commission.
Management Comment
The Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer commented that they are trying to make data gathering as fast as possible to finish the Solid Waste Management Plan for the next ten years.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate at least 1% of their IRA for LCPC and provide programs, projects and activities for the promotion of the rights and welfare of children as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.
Management Comment
The Municipal Budget Officer committed to comply with the recommendation in the ensuing year.
Appraisal Committee to come up with appraised value of those vehicles without valuation for recording purposes.
observations.managementAction: Appraised value of vehicles was established by the appraisal committee

observations.recommendation: General Services Office to include in the RPCPPE the serviceable vehicles and in the IIRUP the unserviceable ones.
observations.managementAction: Updating the RCPPE and IIRUP was made.

observations.recommendation: Municipal Treasurer to register and transfer the above Motor Vehicles in the name of the Municipality in accordance with Section 7 of RA No. 4136 and COA Circular No. 77-6c dated January 24, 1977 and exert full efforts to locate the missing documents to expedite the registration and transfer the same.
observations.managementAction: The agency went to Diliman, Quezon City to ask for the documents of unregistered vehicles but to no avail.
observations.reason: Unavailability of documents necessary for the registration of vehicles in the name of the Municipality. [Reiterated in Observation No. 2, Part II of this report]

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor monitor/facilitate the immediate titling of subject lots in the name of the Municipality to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future and in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: Two (2) titles were processed and copies of the said title were submitted to this office.
observations.reason: Titling and validation of other properties is still ongoing. [Reiterated in Observation No. 1, Part II of this report]

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to record the vehicle received  as donation in the appropriate account in compliance with Section 112 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and PPSAS 17 and 23 for proper presentation in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: A journal entry was made to record the vehicle received as donation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to enforce massive collection of delinquent real property taxes by conducting tax campaign and offering incentives of full payment of tax dues. Require her to exert extra efforts in collecting the delinquent real property taxes and impose the available remedies provided for under the Local Government Code.
observations.managementAction: Efforts to collect delinquent real property taxes were made including sending of Notice of Delinquency to the taxpayers as well as making tax campaign.
observations.reason: Reconciliation between the Municipal Treasurers Office and the Municipal Assessors Office increased the collectibles on real property taxes.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to adopt efficient and aggressive collection strategies to enforce collection of the overdue loan accounts from the concerned beneficiaries to ensure the continuity of the livelihood project and give other prospective beneficiaries equal opportunity in availing capital for the livelihood program. We also reiterated that if all means failed, resort to legal action to avoid loss of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to livelihood beneficiaries , and field visitation was conducted. Staffs were also assigned to collect and monitor payments.
observations.reason: Difficulties in collecting from the debtors because most of the borrowers were not interested in paying their obligations because of the El Niño which severely damaged their crops in the past years. [Reiterated in Observation No. 3, Part II of this report]

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all payments are made by checks payable directly to MERALCO and to deduct taxes due for remittance to BIR pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 and BIR Regulation No. 1-87. We also recommended to refund the amount of Ᵽ5,623.60.
observations.managementAction: The amount of P5,623.60 was already refunded..

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to conduct regular revision of assessment to update current and previous taxable parcels for valuation and tax purposes in compliance with Section 219 of the Local Government Code of 1991. Also, we recommended that the Municipal Assessor make a representation to the Sangguniang Panlalawigan for the earliest approval of their request.
observations.managementAction: The process of conducting the general revision of Real Property Assessment is currently in process. Also, the proposed schedule of market values for land was already reviewed and approved by the Bureau of Local Government and Finance.
observations.reason: Revision of Real Property Assessment is a provincial imposition. [Reiterated in Observation No. 5, Part II of this report]

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete sets of subsidiary ledgers for all economic enterprises operated by the Municipality and prepare the required financial statements and supporting schedules in compliance with Section 313 of RA No. 7160 and Sections 105 and 110 of the MNGAS. We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer adopt other measures that would improve the income generation and ensure that collections/ income are properly accounted for.
observations.managementAction: The financial statements were prepared by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: Lack of Sangguniang Bayan (SB) Resolution for the creation of economic enterprise. [Reiterated in Observation No. 7, Part II of this report]

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local Development Council, the MPDC Office and the Budget Office to comply with the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% development fund in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 in order to provide the constituents of the Municipality the immediate benefits that may be derived from the developmental projects and programs prescribed in the said Joint Memorandum Circular. We also recommended to refund the above expenditures of Ᵽ244,973.57 that were not in accordance with the guidelines of the 20% development fund.
observations.managementAction: The management is already cautious in utilizing the 20% development fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (“PPA”) and those that are to be implemented during the year, as of January 1, 2015; and monthly monitoring report in compliance with the above cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The management thru MPDC   is already submitting the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (“PPA”).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Accountant comply with the provisions of COA, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 in the disbursement of Confidential/ Intelligence Fund. We also recommended to submit to the ICFAU the disbursement voucher and its supporting documents covering the expenses of Ᵽ88,438.00.
observations.managementAction: Payment of intelligence and confidential expenses has been stopped.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor thru the LGU GFPS prepare plan and implement programs, projects and activities that are aligned with GAD objectives.
observations.managementAction: Guidelines for the utilization of Gender and Development (GAD) Fund were adhered to.
